title: Automated Benchmark Auditing for AI Agents and Large Language Models

companion_version.comparison_status: Both immutable extractions were compared. The v2 changes are primarily editorial, correct the official repository URL, clarify that Claude Opus 4.6 was used for the BenchGuard comparison, add one citation, and revise wording; no deep methodological or numerical audit is claimed here.

official_release.timing_boundary: The pinned latest commit is dated 2026-05-27T23:03:52Z, after immutable arXiv v2 was updated at 2026-05-26T06:39:24Z. It is the complete official review-time repository snapshot, not demonstrated to be the exact paper-time implementation. GitHub API queries at acquisition returned no tags or releases.
official_release.acquisition_scope: The complete pinned archive was downloaded and ZIP integrity plus its full member table were verified. It contains the auditor implementation, prompts/rubrics, benchmark configurations, audit outputs, validation artifacts, leaderboard matrices, and score-refresh code. No methodological audit, annotation validation, or score recomputation is claimed by this acquisition record.

checks: arXiv API returned immutable v2 metadata and an abstract without a withdrawal or retraction notice
both immutable PDFs report the expected title, complete seven-author byline, and 39 pages
both layout-preserving extractions are non-empty and readable from the title and abstract through the final limitations, ethics, data-provenance, and release-plan sections
the v2 extraction contains 39 form-feed page boundaries, 2360 split lines, and no NUL bytes
the v2 source archive is readable and contains 35 submission members
the complete official pinned release archive passes ZIP integrity checks and contains 4427 files across 4536 ZIP members

claim_boundary: This acquisition creates verifiable immutable v1/v2 full-text, v2 source-archive, and pinned official-release evidence only. It is not a deep review or release audit. No claim is made about issue prevalence, auditor precision or recall, expert-review quality, false-negative rate, task-remediation validity, score or rank sensitivity, benchmark quality, agent capability, professional validity, production fitness, safety, or readiness.
